story.title: From final year to first offer
story.subtitle: A playable career campaign where every step unlocks the next opportunity.

chapters.title: The final semester sprint
chapters.scene: You are one month from finishing school. Marks still matter, but the real game has started: projects, references, proof of skill, and the first serious job hunt.
chapters.briefing: Balance exam pressure with career momentum. Finish the capstone, collect evidence of your work, and protect your consistency meter before burnout starts costing you choices.

beats.title: Submit capstone project
beats.detail: Turn your analytics project into a portfolio-ready case study.

beats.title: Ask lecturer for reference
beats.detail: A strong academic reference can skip one screening stage.

beats.title: Choose final-year focus
beats.detail: Spend focus points on SQL, Python, or stakeholder communication.

chapters.title: Build the career card
chapters.scene: Your CV is your player card. Recruiters will judge your overall rating in seconds, so weak sections need to be upgraded before you enter the job board.
chapters.briefing: Convert school work into business impact. Replace generic claims with evidence, sharpen the skills section, and make one project read like a real workplace result.

beats.title: Write profile summary
beats.detail: Make it specific to data roles instead of sounding like a template.

beats.title: Upgrade project impact
beats.detail: Turn tasks into outcomes, numbers, and tools used.

beats.title: Patch missing keywords
beats.detail: Add role-specific keywords without stuffing the CV.

chapters.title: Enter the opportunity market
chapters.scene: The board opens like a transfer market. Some roles fit your current build. Others are tempting, but applying too early can burn time and confidence.
chapters.briefing: Scout roles by match percentage, salary range, skill gap, and application cost. The smart play is not applying everywhere, it is applying where your story lands.

beats.title: Shortlist three roles
beats.detail: Pick a safe role, a reach role, and a wild-card role.

beats.title: Compare skill gaps
beats.detail: Use the board to decide what to improve before applying.

beats.title: Save one dream company
beats.detail: Long-term goals become future season content.

chapters.title: Application matchday
chapters.scene: Every application is a match. Your CV, cover note, timing, and referrals all affect the result before a human ever reads the full page.
chapters.briefing: Prepare a tailored application. Spend effort where it matters: role fit, proof of impact, and a short message that sounds human.

beats.title: Tailor CV to selected role
beats.detail: Move the most relevant project into the first scan zone.

beats.title: Write recruiter note
beats.detail: Keep it short, specific, and tied to the company problem.

beats.title: Submit before deadline
beats.detail: Late applications lose momentum and referral bonuses.

chapters.title: The interview gauntlet
chapters.scene: The interview is not one boss fight. It is a series: recruiter screen, technical task, culture conversation, and salary expectations.
chapters.briefing: Prepare stories, practice SQL pressure questions, and decide what salary range you can defend without sounding random.

beats.title: Practice technical challenge
beats.detail: SQL joins, messy data, and explaining trade-offs under time pressure.

beats.title: Prepare story bank
beats.detail: Use STAR stories for conflict, learning, and delivery.

beats.title: Set salary anchor
beats.detail: Know market range before the recruiter asks.

chapters.title: Negotiate the first contract
chapters.scene: The offer arrives. It feels like the finish line, but now the salary, growth path, manager quality, and learning curve all matter.
chapters.briefing: Compare the offer like a pro. Counter respectfully, check the growth path, and decide whether this is a launchpad or a trap.

beats.title: Read total package
beats.detail: Base pay, benefits, training budget, remote policy, and probation terms.

beats.title: Run negotiation mini-game
beats.detail: Counter too high and risk tension. Accept too fast and leave value behind.

beats.title: Start first job season
beats.detail: Unlock tickets, teammates, reviews, and production consequences.

jobBoard.title: Graduate Data Analyst
jobBoard.hook: Strong fit for your Excel, SQL, and capstone project. Communication score is the promotion path.

jobBoard.title: Junior Data Engineer
jobBoard.hook: A reach role with pipeline exposure. Python needs one more proof point before applying.

jobBoard.title: Business Intelligence Intern
jobBoard.hook: Lower title, strong brand signal. Could become a reputation play if you perform well.

sections.name: Profile
sections.note: Good opening, needs sharper role targeting.

sections.name: Projects
sections.note: Capstone needs measurable impact and tools used.

sections.name: Skills
sections.note: SQL and Excel are clear. Python needs proof.

sections.name: Experience
sections.note: Use school, freelance, and volunteer work as evidence.

review.managerNote: The next unlock is not more motivation. It is a stronger story, cleaner CV evidence, and smarter applications.
